findings.description: Header seal/logo graphics are not preserved in the HTML rendering.
findings.evidence: On page 1, the PDF shows the North Carolina state seal at the top center and a DEQ logo near the bottom left. In the HTML, these graphics are omitted and replaced by text only.

findings.description: Some text emphasis and alignment from the letterhead/header are weakened in HTML.
findings.evidence: On page 1, the PDF uses distinct placement and styling for the left header names/titles, centered agency heading, and underlined 'Certified Mail' / 'Return Receipt Requested'. In the HTML these appear as plain linear text with reduced visual distinction.

findings.description: Signature graphic is missing from the HTML rendering.
findings.evidence: On page 2, the PDF contains a handwritten signature above 'Judith A. Wehner'. In the HTML, only the typed name/title remain.

findings.description: Centered address block and signature block formatting are simplified in HTML.
findings.evidence: On page 2, the PDF centers the mailing address block and signature section, while the HTML converts them into left-aligned running text, reducing visual structure but not losing content.

findings.description: The HTML includes fabricated content in the page title and injected stylesheet/theme code that do not appear in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML contains '<title>Converted Document</title>' and CSS comments/text such as 'Digital Commons NC — Standalone Theme CSS' plus a Google Fonts import. The PDF is a two-page DEQ letter and does not contain this title or any stylesheet/theme text.

findings.description: The HTML does not use heading elements to reflect the document structure; no h1-h6 headings are present.
findings.evidence:
The PDF has clear structural elements such as the date 'January 9, 2020', subject line 'RE: Proposed Alamance Quarry and Construction Materials Quarry', and page-two continuation header 'Certified Mail / Mr. Threatt / Page Two'. In HTML, all content is placed in

tags with no heading hierarchy.

findings.description: Image alt text is generic/incomplete and does not fully describe the actual page images.
findings.evidence: HTML alt text for page 1 is 'North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality official letterhead' and for page 2 is 'Page 2 of the official letter'. The images represent full document pages, not just a letterhead or generic page marker.
